children.heading: .—Establishment and Designation
children.label: a .—Establishment and Designation
children.text: There is a Federal Judiciary Building in Washington, D.C., known and designated as the “Thurgood Marshall Federal Judiciary Building”.

children.heading: .—Squares 721 and 722
children.label: 1 .—Squares 721 and 722
children.text: Title to squares 721 and 722 remains in the Federal Government.

children.heading: .—Building
children.label: 2 .—Building
children.text: Title to the Building and other improvements constructed or otherwise made immediately reverts to the Government at the expiration of not more than 30 years from the effective date of the lease agreement referred to in without payment of any compensation by the Government.section 6504 of this title

children.heading: .—Size of building
children.label: 1 .—Size of building
children.text: The Building (excluding parking facilities) may not exceed 520,000 gross square feet in size above the level of Columbia Plaza in the District of Columbia.

children.heading: .—Height of building
children.label: 2 .—Height of building
children.text: The height of the Building and other improvements shall be compatible with the height of surrounding Government and historic buildings and conform to the provisions of the Act of (ch. 263, ) (known as the Building Height Act of 1910).June 1, 191036 Stat. 452

children.label: A
children.text: be designed in harmony with historical and Government buildings in the vicinity;

children.label: B
children.text: reflect the symbolic importance and historic character of the United States Capitol and other buildings on the United States Capitol Grounds; and

children.label: C
children.text: represent the dignity and stability of the Government.

children.heading: .—Approval of Chief Justice
children.label: d .—Approval of Chief Justice
children.text: All final decisions regarding architectural design of the Building are subject to the approval of the Chief Justice.

children.heading: .—Chilled Water and Steam From Capitol Power Plant
children.label: e .—Chilled Water and Steam From Capitol Power Plant
children.text: If the Building is connected with the Capitol Power Plant, the Architect of the Capitol shall furnish chilled water and steam from the Plant to the Building on a reimbursable basis.

children.heading: .—Construction Standards
children.label: f .—Construction Standards
children.text: The Building and other improvements constructed under this chapter shall meet all standards applicable to construction of a federal building.

children.heading: .—Accounting System
children.label: g .—Accounting System
children.text: The Architect shall maintain an accounting system for operation and maintenance of the Building and other improvements which will allow accurate projections of the dates and cost of major repairs, improvements, reconstructions, and replacements of the Building and improvements and other capital expenditures on the Building and improvements.

children.heading: .—Building codes, permits, or inspection
children.label: 1 .—Building codes, permits, or inspection
children.text: The Building is not subject to any law of the District of Columbia relating to building codes, permits, or inspection, including any such law enacted by Congress.

children.heading: .—Taxes
children.label: 2 .—Taxes
children.text: The Building and other improvements constructed under this chapter are not subject to any law of the District of Columbia relating to real estate and personal property taxes, special assessments, or other taxes, including any such law enacted by Congress.
